ActionTaken: No formal action recorded. Potential Northern Section 951 (Mayor Batos) Mayor Batos provided a brief introduction. Don Scott, Executive Director, Lee County Metropolitan Planning Organization (MPO), through a PowerPoint presentation, provided information regarding the status of the CR 951 extension. Council questions and discussion followed. Public Comment: Jim Shields, Grandezza resident, addressed concern regarding home values as long as the extension remained on the long range plan; it should be removed from the plan. Jim Varey, Grandezza resident, representing East Corkscrew Coalition and on behalf of Grandezza residents, addressed concern regarding traffic and decline in property values. Don Eslick, ECCL, provided background information and requested that Council adopt a resolution requesting Lee County MPO remove the portion of proposed CR 951 extension from the 2040 needs plan. Bill Williams, Vice Chairman,
Outcome: No formal action recorded. Potential Northern Section 951 (Mayor Batos) Mayor Batos provided a brief introduction. Don Scott, Executive Director, Lee County Metropolitan Planning Organization (MPO), through a PowerPoint presentation, provided information regarding the status of the CR 951 extension. Council questions and discussion followed. Public Comment: Jim Shields, Grandezza resident, addressed concern regarding home values as long as the extension remained on the long range plan; it should be removed from the plan. Jim Varey, Grandezza resident, representing East Corkscrew Coalition and on behalf of Grandezza residents, addressed concern regarding traffic and decline in property values. Don Eslick, ECCL, provided background information and requested that Council adopt a resolution requesting Lee County MPO remove the portion of proposed CR 951 extension from the 2040 needs plan. Bill Williams, Vice Chairman,

Vice Mayor Ribble would like to add the intersection Meadowbrook and Coconut to the study. Councilmember Zalucki would like to add the intersections Corkscrew and Estero Crossing Blvd and Corkscrew and Bella Terra Blvd to the study

Vice Mayor Ribble would like to add the intersection Meadowbrook and Coconut to the study. Councilmember Zalucki would like to add the intersections Corkscrew and Estero Crossing Blvd and Corkscrew and Bella Terra Blvd to the study
MotionText: Approved Contract EC 2024-07 with Johnson Engineering, Inc. to prepare the Village Wide Traffic Study for $249,480, approve a $25,000 contingency for additional services that may be required to complete the project, authorize the Village Manager to sign the contract and other additional implementing documents within the scope of the contract on behalf of the Village of Estero Council, and authorize the Village Manager to execute an amendment to the agreement to add the locations added by council. Presentation by: Norman Trebilcock, Trebilcock Consulting Solutions, PA, provided details of the approximate 3 Yz mile study area, which examined existing and future conditions; traffic counts collected during the peak season 2016; trips added from future developments directly impacting Coconut Road in Bonita Springs, unincorporated Lee County and the Village of Estero; and analyzed the intersection of US 41 and Coconut Road in terms of delay based on vehicle turning movement counts. Mr. Trebilcock also addressed study findings and recommendations. Questions or Comments by: Councilmembers Boesch, Errington, Ribble, Vice Mayor Levitan, and Mayor Batos. Discussion included cooperation between entities and potential impacts of future construction projects. Public

Summary: DISCUSSION - Welcome to Estero Signs Mayor Batos mentioned past discussions about placing "gateway" signs at the northern and southern sections of US 41, on Three Oaks and Corkscrew, and in a few other areas, and asked whether a committee should be formed. Action: No formal action recorded. DISCUSSION - Welcome to Estero Signs Mayor Batos mentioned past discussions about placing "gateway" signs at the northern and southern sections of US 41, on Three Oaks and Corkscrew, and in a few other are...
ActionTaken: No formal action recorded. DISCUSSION - Welcome to Estero Signs Mayor Batos mentioned past discussions about placing "gateway" signs at the northern and southern sections of US 41, on Three Oaks and Corkscrew, and in a few other areas, and asked whether a committee should be formed to pursue the project, Discussion followed regarding the availability of grant funds, jurisdiction over median locations, the need for a Village logo, the concept of a logo design contest, and locations of existing signs, There was consensus to postpone consideration of new signs. The Mayor called for public input; and the following individual came forward: Old Estero resident, Patty Whitehead - Logo design contest and other possible design sources
Outcome: No formal action recorded. DISCUSSION - Welcome to Estero Signs Mayor Batos mentioned past discussions about placing "gateway" signs at the northern and southern sections of US 41, on Three Oaks and Corkscrew, and in a few other areas, and asked whether a committee should be formed to pursue the project, Discussion followed regarding the availability of grant funds, jurisdiction over median locations, the need for a Village logo, the concept of a logo design contest, and locations of existing signs, There was consensus to postpone consideration of new signs. The Mayor called for public input; and the following individual came forward: Old Estero resident, Patty Whitehead - Logo design contest and other possible design sources
